function.name: plan_write
function.description: Create a plan for a task. MUST be the first tool call when receiving a new task with task_id. Writes all step specifications at once. After this call, begin execution by step_read(step_n=1).

task_id.description: Unique task identifier, format 't-NNNN' or similar, provided by user.

goal.description: One-sentence high-level goal of the task.

steps.description: Ordered list of step specifications (minimum 2 steps).

preconds.description: Conditions that must hold before this step starts (e.g., 'step 1 DONE').

checklist.description: Points to verify during/after the step. Used for SELF-CHECK.

action_hint.description: Which tool and with what shape of args is expected for this step.

success_criteria.description: How to decide the step is DONE.

function.name: step_read
function.description: Read the full specification of a single step. MUST be called before executing any project-tool action for that step. Do not rely on memory — always read.

function.name: step_update_result
function.description: Record the outcome of a step. MUST be called after executing the step's action and performing SELF-CHECK against the step checklist. Do not proceed to the next step without this call.

status.description: DONE = success_criteria met. FAILED = irrecoverable. NEEDS_REPLAN = need to adjust plan and try different approach.

result.description: One-sentence outcome of the step, includes evidence (e.g., 'file found at path X').

notes.description: Optional extra context, especially for FAILED / NEEDS_REPLAN.

function.name: task_status
function.description: Get an overview of the task: goal, current step, total steps, brief status of each. Use when re-orienting after a long pause or after plan_revise.

function.name: plan_revise
function.description: Replace the tail of the plan starting from from_step. Use AFTER step_update_result with status NEEDS_REPLAN, to adjust subsequent steps in light of new information.

from_step.description: The step number to start the replacement from. Steps before this are preserved.

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read the full text content of a file in the project. Use for examining existing code before making edits.

function.name: list_dir
function.description: List entries (files and subdirs) in a project directory. Use for verifying paths before read_file / search_and_replace.

function.name: search_and_replace
function.description: Replace the first occurrence of old_text with new_text in the file at path. Returns matches count. If matches=0, old_text was not found — do NOT retry with the same arguments; replan instead.

old_text.description: Exact substring to find (include sufficient context — 2-4 lines — for uniqueness).

new_text.description: Replacement substring. Preserve indentation and surrounding context.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Create a new file with the given content, or overwrite an existing one. Use this for NEW files: new source files, new tests, new documentation. For editing EXISTING files prefer search_and_replace — it's safer and won't destroy unrelated content.

path.description: Project-relative path of the file to write.

content.description: Full text content of the file.
